Kudos for @Dawson Carter who spun up a task alerting dashboard to monitor daily refresh of data in the semantic layer that will be able to send alerts to end users when data appears stale. He pulled this together in an afternoon and presented it the following morning! A quote from a technical stakeholder (Santosh, the Director of Applications at Centric Brands) "The dashboard looks awesome, by the way. Great work!" :taco: :taco::taco::taco::taco: He is planning on taking this to other teams and requesting they implement something similar. Dawson is a Sigma wizard :male_mage::skin-tone-2:

:turbotax: *SO MANY MVPS on the TurboTax CJA project this week* :taco:
• @Jefferson Williams has been everywhere all at once solving our toughest pipeline & QA issues. If there's an award for digging into "why" something is broken, Jeff is the winner :trophy:
• I don't know that I've seen anyone ramp up quicker than @Peyton Anderson taking over the Data Dictionary Work stream this week. She truly stepped up to work through tedious issues, keep the project moving, and create a streamlined work back plan that will keep us ahead of deadline. :party_unicorn:
• @Luke Youtsey Was able to pick up a slow-moving work stream, assess why it went sideways, and get it back on track, all while covering for Berg on his other tax season projects and prepping for PTO. I've never seen someone document their PTO support plan so thoroughly :fire_engine:

• I'm not sure how @Victoria Stephen does it all :cat_typing: She keeps track of a million small issues _and_ works through the most challenge CJA problems. I don't know if I've ever admired someone's work ethic _and_ attitude so much. 
• Honorable mentions to Alanna for picking up SO MANY ad hoc requests this week & Ruturaj for consistent CJA research into the art of the possible.

:taco: Huge shoutout to @Victor Valdes Diaz for showing poise under pressure :ice_cube:\n\nThere was an integration issue between TT and CK that was causing TT starts to not be identifiable by the CK team when they are optimizing their campaigns. The impact of this was making Cost-Per-Click for their PPC campaigns around $120, the expected CPC post-fix would be closer to $20. After some lengthy troubleshooting with executives, marketing managers and data engineers, we got a patch stood up and send out in a matter of hours. A high visibility win on a big initiative during the most intense time of the year is always a nice way to cap off the week. Thank you for making us look like superheroes :superhero:

Kroger is one of those clients where our stakeholders regularly see the value we bring, but the “peer” level employees we work with on teams are typically consultant-adverse. Today we got an email from one of those “peers” who wanted to let us know he was leaving the org and wanted to stay connected. This is a huge nod to the work @Brandon Martinez and @Alex Krumins have done not only on thoughtful technical solutions, but also fostering relationships where teams truly enjoy working with us. Thanks Brandon & Alex for building a culture of trust and collaboration with the Kroger team - I don’t think Russell is the only one who feels this way. :taco::taco:

:taco: Shout-out to @Peyton Anderson! When you're a junior analyst listening to a more seasoned teammate walk through their logic, it's really easy to just nod your head and go with it. Well, lucky for me and our client, Peyton isn't that person. I was explaining how I might join some tables to get a specific cut of the data, and she stopped me: "But I don't think that actually answers the question Wenyi has for tomorrow. That'll help us for next week's asks, but we need a different look for tomorrow." She was absolutely right. This is _the_ fundamental question we try to get analysts in the habit of asking: "Does this actually answer what we're being asked?" And she's got it down. Love to see that kind of thinking. Way to go, Peyton.:fire:

:taco:  @Abhiram Nannapaneni @Olga Poleleyeva @Candice Dominguez @Jefferson Williams deserve all the shoutouts for your work throughout the season with the TurboTax Marketing Data as a Product work

Our client did a readout for some mid season impact metrics and in short, the results were :banana:s
• A total of $92M in spend efficiencies as a direct result of the work that we did to tune their MMM
    ◦ $76M of this was in streamlining paid media data and standardizing analysis cuts across channels
    ◦ $16M of this was in PPC spend optimizations by cutting the data hourly instead of daily to allow for more granular bidding strategies
• 35% increase in users for their Executive Command Center, which is fueled by the data we process
    ◦ 71% increase in sessions for users that were logging in due to increased data quality and reduced SLAs
Brandon was not subtle about giving credit to this team and was open about how much he enjoys working with Concord. These types of numbers are mind blowing and what keep us pushing our clients into the future :flying_saucer:

TurboTax came to us asking if we can stand up a process that takes requests in a google form from page owners who want to display reviews on their sites, evaluates customer reviews to determine which would be the best fit to show for those specific pages and output a list of suggested reviews, then there would be an added incentive if we could use an LLM :galaxy-brain:

This is exactly the type of work that helps us break into new capabilities and extend the analysis into multi-year commitments

Super interesting work and executed with precision, much appreciated :orangejustice:

I’ve long said that only a small portion of what we do as consultants is the actual work. I believe that one of the larger responsibilities we take on is in building and maintaining relationships. It is in building trust, earning respect, and making our stakeholders shine in their roles. If we fail to honor these responsibilities, it doesn’t matter how smart we are or good we are at solving problems. Our abilities get us in the door. Being good humans keeps us in the conversation.

With this in mind, I’m offering my last :taco: for today to @Chase Porter. It’s been a couple of years since he last worked with Wellmark, but his name came up a couple of times in conversation with Wellmark’s CMO today. She praised how he understood their needs and recommended solutions, meeting them where they were. She appreciated his ability to communicate without arrogance, empowering them along the way.

Thanks for leaving such a positive impression, @Chase Porter! We’re still building on the foundation you set!

Wanted to give a big shout out to @Sam Varón, @Steph Schaller, @Michelle Vega,  @Anna Trenary & @Kristin Turner (and more) for the awesome work being done on Sally Beauty :sally-beauty:. Today they had their Q3 earnings call in which the CEO called out directly how the personalization efforts were driving revenue growth for the entire organization. In fact, the word "Personalization" was mentioned *SIX* times during the earnings call.

From CEO: _"The combination of marketplaces, licensed colors on demand, and innovation, as well as *personalization* and enhanced performance marketing contributed approximately two ninety basis points of comp sales growth in the third quarter and two fifty points year to date"_

From CFO: "_Yeah, on the gross margin front in terms of Sally, very pleased with the performance there. I’m getting the planned and track the benefits from our field for growth effort in terms of how we’re redeploying that to drive that consistent traffic. It’s really our marketing efforts, CRM, our *personalization*, and then bringing innovation. So feel very good about continuing to maintain that profile as well as the traffic driving activities that we have that we’re deploying going forward."_

Excited for what this team can continue to accomplish as we finish out the FY and move into FY26 starting in October. Oh, and paired with the earnings call was a nice bump to their stock price as well :chart_with_upwards_trend:. :taco: :taco: Wanted to give a massive kudos to @Scott Sanders for an absolutely masterful client interaction this morning (all over slack nonetheless)! :trophy:

What started as a challenging conversation - with the client adamantly insisting on an approach that didn't make sense mathematically - turned into a brilliant example of collaborative problem-solving. Scott didn't just push back or shut down the client's perspective. Instead, he demonstrated incredible patience, communication skills, and technical expertise.

Through a combination of active listening, clear explanation, and strategic communication, he guided the client to understand our proposed solution. The most impressive part? The client didn't just reluctantly agree - they ended up praising our team for helping them see a more effective approach!

This is how you turn a potential roadblock into a relationship-building moment. Scott didn't just solve a technical challenge; he elevated our entire team's reputation in the process.

Truly exceptional work that embodies the best of our collaborative spirit! So happy I have the opportunity to learn from you! :clapping_all:

Two weeks ago, @Scott Sanders and I were pulled into a meeting with about 15 other analysts and PMs at Intuit. During this meeting, an analyst walked through their IXP plan for a set of crucial genAI experiments that are going to run all year. Scott quickly realized there was a major flaw in their set-up which would render reading the experiments cleanly nearly impossible. Scott reached out to the analyst afterwards and got a conversation going both on slack and on zoom meetings to clarify what he thought was the issue was actually an issue (this took hours on multiple days on Scott's part) and then provided a detailed  work-around on how to solve it. So many people had looked at this IXP plan and had not noticed there was an issue! When this was brought up to the attention of our stakeholder, she paused and said  "I really appreciate the diligence you took in this matter. You noticed something the rest of us glazed over.” This kind of attention to detail, even when we aren't specifically being asked to solve a problem, is what makes Scott a spectacular data scientist and consultant! Great work, Scott! :taco:

Tacos for @Brian Davis and @Gressi Lopez for their work automating the WBR (Weekly Business Review) report for :intuit: CS! When we were introduced to this report it lived in a google sheet with I'll estimate somewhere between 20-40 people jumping in and making edits every week with very little process around it to manage changes (regularly adding new metrics) or enforce data governance (hint: accidental edits to calculations) and using only one live doc (no working vs final copy) - it was easy to understand why this report was a pain point for the Service Analytics team, especially since it has a lot of leadership visibility.

Enter Brian and Gressi.

Brian's organization around this report has been a lifesaver - keeping up with all the changes still going on in their google sheet report, pulling necessary updates into the Qlik dashboard and finding creative solves in Qlik for metrics with unique data source challenges :galaxy-brain:

And Gressi jumped on this project and picked up data source details from Jess to create a unified data source and got a v1 of this Qlik dash built out EARLY even with limited experience with Qlik prior to this project :qlik-cowboy:

We used to hear about our stakeholder for this report working weekends trying to manage all the changes going on in this dash, but today we heard how much he's actually getting to enjoy his weekends and relax and he's really excited for our dashboard review this week. Thank you so much for your work to give this report a full makeover! :taco:

@Jacob Logan was asked by ICS VEP to use the new Hierarchical Bayesian notebooks to size for a global holdout for their experimentation program.  He found when doing this that the notebook crashed.  Every. Single. Time.   The code he was running required hundreds of simulations using large datasets with over 300 billion (yes you read that) simulated records. He diagnosed the issue as the cluster running out of memory and refactored the code to be much more resource efficient and faster allowing for the analysis to be completed!  Taco this man! :taco:

Major shoutout to @Scott Sanders and @John Carney :taco::taco: for their instrumental roles in the creation and validation of the Bayesian hierarchical tools for Intuit's expert experimentation! Our Inuit stakeholders just previewed this work to a wider audience of director, PMs, and analysts and it was received with much enthusiasm! I didn't have time to document all the positive comments, but some highlights were:
• "[Concord Team] you have done an amazing job and I can't thank you enough for all the energy that went into this work"
• "These tools are empowering us to be better analysts and make better decisions for Intuit”
• "This is so exciting for us to unleash more tests within expert spaces"
Scott and John drove this project in so many ways. They continued to push on developing this suite of tools when I had to unexpectedly be out for several weeks, they've made the Bayesian framework and its nuances easier to digest. Additionally, Scott and John have ensured my growth on this project by approaching our meetings with a teaching mindset. This project has not been easy, but working with the both of them has made it enjoyable and rewarding. Thank you, Scott and John for your effort, attitude, and attention to detail on this project!

I’ve written before about our participation in the Google Enhanced Conversions program, the brands it exposes us to and the work it has generated for the Analytics Development team.  Most of our engagements there are straightforward, new implementations.  Sometimes, however, we are called in to debug troubled sites that client, Google, and other teams have not been able to resolve.  The Disney engagement from this past spring is an example of that, and it helped cast Concord as the people who can fix the weird stuff.

A more recent example is Banco Itau, a Brazilian bank that had been struggling - along with Google - to understand why their EC data was not getting sent.  The debugging sessions were made more challenging by the language barrier, with the Google reps providing the English<->Portuguese translations.  @John Hartert worked with the invovled parties, looking “outside the square” at possible problems, helping the team to a resolution.

The following message was shared by Banco Itau’s account rep with the Google team that coordinates our work with them.  When you read the word “Concord,” substitute “John Hartert”:

_“Thank you for the connection with Concord - that was a big help for us indeed! We could collect evidence with them to understand the issue with Itau. Corrections are now in progress and we are properly implementing EC on Itaú!”_

Way to go, John - although I am certain this will lead to getting referrals to even weirder problems to solve!  :taco: :taco: :taco: :taco: :taco:

_*Subject:* Appreciation for Concord's Partnership - THANK YOU!_
 
_Dear Smilen,_
 
_I wanted to take a moment to express our sincere gratitude to Concord for partnering with RYFFC. Your willingness to collaborate with us during a time of such fragility and resource scarcity is truly commendable._
 
_Since the project kick-off, Concord has consistently exceeded our expectations. We were initially focused on simply maintaining operations, but thanks to your flexible model, we've achieved significant mobile development milestones. Your world-class team, led by your exceptional leadership, has been instrumental in our success._
 
_Tsvetan's genius expertise, Vasil's unwavering dedication, humor, and cultural fit, and Stoyan's innovative and scalable approach have been invaluable. The addition of Petar’s deep subject matter knowledge has further solidified our belief that our partnership with Concord is the ideal fit for both present and future endeavors._
 
_As RYFFC continues to grow, we are confident that Concord will be an integral part of our journey. We look forward to a long and prosperous partnership, and we will not hesitate to advocate for Concord's resources within our professional network._
 
_Thank you again for believing in RYFFC. We are excited about the future of our collaboration._
 
_Sincerely,_
 
_Collin Mothupi_
_Co-Founder@RYFFC_

This is a huge nod to the work @Brandon Martinez and @Alex Krumins have done not only on thoughtful technical solutions, but also fostering relationships where teams truly enjoy working with us. Thanks Brandon & Alex for building a culture of trust and collaboration with the Kroger team - I don’t think Russell is the only one who feels this way. :taco::taco:

:taco:  @Abhiram Nannapaneni @Olga Poleleyeva @Candice Dominguez @Jefferson Williams deserve all the shoutouts for your work throughout the season with the TurboTax Marketing Data as a Product team

Our client did a readout for some mid season impact metrics and in short, the results were :banana:s
• A total of $92M in spend efficiencies as a direct result of the work that we did to tune their MMM
    ◦ $76M of this was in streamlining paid media data and standardizing analysis cuts across channels
    ◦ $16M of this was in PPC spend optimizations by cutting the data hourly instead of daily to allow for more granular bidding strategies
• 35% increase in users for their Executive Command Center, which is fueled by the data we process
    ◦ 71% increase in sessions for users that were logging in due to increased data quality and reduced SLAs
Brandon was not subtle about giving credit to this team and was open about how much he enjoys working with Concord. These types of numbers are mind blowing and what keep us pushing our clients into the future :flying_saucer:

Shoutout to @Jefferson Williams for crushing the gen ai game :taco:

TurboTax came to us asking if we can stand up a process that takes requests in a google form  page owners who want to display reviews on their sites, evaluates customer reviews to determine which would be the best fit to show for those specific pages and output a list of suggested reviews, then there would be an added incentive if we could use an LLM :galaxy-brain:

When walking through the first pass of workflow, our stakeholder was blown away. He talked about how interesting it was to see the LLM ranking and justification fields to make the results more objective, which can serve as the foothold he needs to scale adoption across all of his page owners that are using our process. This is exactly the type of work that helps us break into new capabilities and extend the analysis into multi-year commitments

Super interesting work and executed with precision, much appreciated :orangejustice:

For the Analytics Development team, the Google partnership is keeping us busy so far in 2026:

• Ongoing Enhanced Conversions (EC) and Google Tag Gateway (GTG) work, with anywhere  5 - 10 new projects coming in weekly (49 new projects in 2026; 295 total non-GIPs projects opened since May 2025).
• A new GCS Implementation Partners (GIPs) program kicked off in late-January, requiring a new outreach structure as we’re responsible not only for _doing_ the EC and GTG work, but leading the sales effort in partnership with Google sellers. The current beta period includes 49 advertisers. Quarterly lead lists are expected to include 100+ new advertisers.
• Expanding EC and GTG work to include Google’s EMEA advertisers has begun. We’re expecting 20 new projects in the coming days, with the first 7 having come in just this morning.
• We learned this week that 2025's Shopify program is renewing for 2026. Last year we managed ~900 individual projects (completing approximately 35%, exceeding targets) for Shopify Plus customers. For 2026 we’ll target non-Plus advertisers. We expect less volume (more customers, but fewer will qualify for Google’s program due to smaller ad spend) but even 50% is 400+ individual projects in Q2-Q3.

Passing along client kudos  a dashboard share out with the QB Payments team

"A big shoutout to the Concord team for all the incredible work to build out this dashboard, and Chad for connecting us with such a great partner"
"We finally have a view stitching together data across all our marketing efforts"

Kudos for @Gabby Lopez for creating visually clean, easy to use dashboard views in qlik and identifying new ways of monitoring qlik refreshes to ensure we have consistently fresh data powering this dashboard

Kudos for @Jacob Logan for walking through some very nuanced campaign attribution logic - this was challenging  requirements gathering to the technical implementation and you did an excellent job walking a non-technical group through the important call outs for these breakouts in the dashboard (especially knowing these are probably the most valuable breakouts in the dash for this team)

Kudos for @Katie Young for her support all across the development process - when we needed extra qlik support you picked it up incredibly quickly and plugged right in alongside Gabby, when we needed extra DE support you quickly built familiarity with Intuit's data and their DE tools and plugged in there. It's been invaluable to have you flex across all this dashboard work!

Wonderful to see Chad nodding along all throughout your presentation and see our team make a great impression on their marketing VP KK who made time to engage in this dashboard review! :taco::quickbooks-qb-cowboy:

I have a whole host of kudos to give for the David Yurman Marketing Analytics expansion :party_parrot: \n\n... First up is @Sean Miller for recognizing the opportunity after a BI scope and driving the opportunity forward :taco:

Wanted to give another big shoutout to the Sally Beauty team on another successful quarter @Sam Varón @Steph Schaller @Rashmi Sandilya @Vaishnava @Liz King @Nick Bade @Kristin Turner @Anna Trenary (too many people to give tacos to). Everyone has been working very hard, and not without some stress and late nights. But, over the course of the last year, we've been able to make serious progress towards the lofty goals that were set before us.

Q4 earnings report came out this morning and we are continuing to see how our work is directly impacting their financial success. Q4 (calendar Q3) Sally Beauty eComm sales are up 23% and their B2B brand eComm sales are up 8%. There were two quotes  the CEO that I think sum up the amazing work that this team has been putting in. She first said, unprompted:

• _I think if we step back, we just feel really good about the momentum in the business with the strategic initiatives driving over 250 basis points of comp this past year and those continuing into the new year. Focused on innovation, performance marketing, personalization, licensed colorist on demand._
Then, in response to this question  an investor: _Can you talk about the headwinds that you're seeing in Q1 and then how you plan to build over the course of the year to give you the confidence despite the volatile backdrop?_ The CEO responded:

• _I think first and foremost, when we think about Q1, I want to reemphasize we feel great about the underlying momentum in the business. What we've seen in terms of licensed colorist on demand, innovation, performance marketing, personalization, we think are all very strong... In terms of pacing and how we see the progression of impact, right, I think what's important to think about is as we go into 2027 and 2028, what customer activation can do for us and this understanding of the customer, being able to respond to that, incorporating how artificial intelligence can help us on that curve. We think the impact in personalization, performance marketing, and our LCOD campaign, I can't overstate the opportunity that we see there in what we're working on and delivering._
This is a direct testament to the hard-work the folks on the Sally scope have been putting in. Congrats on another successful quarter!

Wanted to give a big shout out to @Sam Varón, @Steph Schaller, @Michelle Vega,  @Anna Trenary & @Kristin Turner (and more) for the awesome work being done on Sally Beauty :sally-beauty:. Today they had their Q3 earnings call in which the CEO called out directly how the personalization efforts were driving revenue growth for the entire organization. In fact, the word "Personalization" was mentioned SIX times during the earnings call.

From CEO: _"The combination of marketplaces, licensed colors on demand, and innovation, as well as personalization and enhanced performance marketing contributed approximately two ninety basis points of comp sales growth in the third quarter and two fifty points year to date"_

From CFO: "_Yeah, on the gross margin front in terms of Sally, very pleased with the performance there. I’m getting the planned and track the benefits  our field for growth effort in terms of how we’re redeploying that to drive that consistent traffic. It’s really our marketing efforts, CRM, our personalization, and then bringing innovation. So feel very good about continuing to maintain that profile as well as the traffic driving activities that we have that we’re deploying going forward."_

Excited for what this team can continue to accomplish as we finish out the FY and move into FY26 starting in October. Oh, and paired with the earnings call was a nice bump to their stock price as well :chart_with_upwards_trend:. :rocket: HUGE shoutout to the RT Integration + Data teams on a successful go-live for Project FLOW! :tada:
This project was a huge lift over the past year, and the team delivered some foundational tech that’s going to set RT up for long-term success. A few highlights:
• A new event-based architecture with all MuleSoft APIs re-designed for scalability
• A full SDLC process with templates to bring consistency across projects
• The launch of LENS, a centralized logging dashboard giving RT visibility across all integrations (a first!)
• Strengthened QA processes, including performance testing and a path toward future automation
• (In flight) A new Unity Catalog built in Databricks to organize and govern data access more effectively

:taco: :taco: Wanted to give a massive kudos to @Scott Sanders for an absolutely masterful client interaction this morning (all over slack nonetheless)! :trophy:

What started as a challenging conversation - with the client adamantly insisting on an approach that didn't make sense mathematically - turned into a brilliant example of collaborative problem-solving. Scott didn't just push back or shut down the client's perspective. Instead, he demonstrated incredible patience, communication skills, and technical expertise.

Through a combination of active listening, clear explanation, and strategic communication, he guided the client to understand our proposed solution. The most impressive part? The client didn't just reluctantly agree - they ended up praising our team for helping them see a more effective approach!

This is how you turn a potential roadblock into a relationship-building moment. Scott didn't just solve a technical challenge; he elevated our entire team's reputation in the process.

Truly exceptional work that embodies the best of our collaborative spirit! So happy I have the opportunity to learn  you! :clapping_all:

Tacos for @Brian Davis and @Gressi Lopez for their work automating the WBR (Weekly Business Review) report for :intuit: CS! When we were introduced to this report it lived in a google sheet with I'll estimate somewhere between 20-40 people jumping in and making edits every week with very little process around it to manage changes (regularly adding new metrics) or enforce data governance (hint: accidental edits to calculations) and using only one live doc (no working vs final copy) - it was easy to understand why this report was a pain point for the Service Analytics team, especially since it has a lot of leadership visibility.

Enter Brian and Gressi.

Brian's organization around this report has been a lifesaver - keeping up with all the changes still going on in their google sheet report, pulling necessary updates into the Qlik dashboard and finding creative solves in Qlik for metrics with unique data source challenges :galaxy-brain:

And Gressi jumped on this project and picked up data source details  Jess to create a unified data source and got a v1 of this Qlik dash built out EARLY even with limited experience with Qlik prior to this project :qlik-cowboy:

We used to hear about our stakeholder for this report working weekends trying to manage all the changes going on in this dash, but today we heard how much he's actually getting to enjoy his weekends and relax and he's really excited for our dashboard review this week. Thank you so much for your work to give this report a full makeover! :taco:

I was part of a QA Summit today and after listening to Steven Knopf I felt so inspired to share. I often struggle to convey the true benefits of having a Quality Engineer on a project. I often feel like people think it is just my “ways of working” and not a normal part of the role of a QE. Steven broke it out so well when he discussed the “Six Other Stances of a QE”.

1. Testing Mentor: use your expertise to help guide the whole team in better understanding proper testing
2. Quality Coach: continuously ask probing questions around impact and improvements
3. Engineer: understand the technology and architecture to be able to contribute and suggest strategies
4. Lifecycle Cultivator: advise on release strategies and when to shift left or right
5. Systems Thinker: have a wider view of the project, understand systems end to end
6. Voice of the Customer: (my favorite) not just testing but representing the user in testing and ensure the client’s project is meeting expectations
Just wanted to share my passion for all things processes and testing, and the value QE brings to a team.
Here is the link to Steven's LinkedIn if you would like to follow and learn more: <https://www.linkedin.com/in/stevenknopf/?originalSubdomain=uk

This is late coming but a big ol’ kudos to @Josh Stephens for detecting and diagnosing underlying issues in key KPI used by ICS.   Josh noticed that aggregated tables were reporting larger denominators than the reported handled count for model based  metrics (i.e., imputed metric scores).  He went back to the fact table and found that rather than being dropped, null model scores were being assigned as negative interactions, depressing the derived metrics scores.  Way to go in improving Intuit’s ability to make informed decisions off their data.

I’ve written before about our participation in the Google Enhanced Conversions program, the brands it exposes us to and the work it has generated for the Analytics Development team.  Most of our engagements there are straightforward, new implementations.  Sometimes, however, we are called in to debug troubled sites that client, Google, and other teams have not been able to resolve.  The Disney engagement  this past spring is an example of that, and it helped cast Concord as the people who can fix the weird stuff.

A more recent example is Banco Itau, a Brazilian bank that had been struggling - along with Google - to understand why their EC data was not getting sent.  The debugging sessions were made more challenging by the language barrier, with the Google reps providing the English<->Portuguese translations.  @John Hartert worked with the invovled parties, looking “outside the square” at possible problems, helping the team to a resolution.

The following message was shared by Banco Itau’s account rep with the Google team that coordinates our work with them.  When you read the word “Concord,” substitute “John Hartert”:

_“Thank you for the connection with Concord - that was a big help for us indeed! We could collect evidence with them to understand the issue with Itau. Corrections are now in progress and we are properly implementing EC on Itaú!”_

Way to go, John - although I am certain this will lead to getting referrals to even weirder problems to solve!  :taco: :taco: :taco: :taco: :taco:

Hey all -- campaign update here (and unfortunately it's not a super exciting one). I've made the decision that the current arc (Dal Boras) will be, at least for the foreseeable future, the final one that we run in this campaign. It's been a good run (over a year :exploding_head:) for the Wildguard and I'm really sad to see it come to a close. It wasn't a decision made lightly. We will continue through the Dal Boras arc -- however many sessions that takes.

Now, this doesn't mean that we stop D&D altogether -- if someone else wants to hop in the drivers seat and run a short campaign or one-shot, you should absolutely do that! I'm happy to help and share how I write, plan, and prep sessions for anyone that wants to learn. Otherwise, we can just do one-shots whenever it's convenient and works with people's schedules. Thanks for all of the effort that you guys have put in over the last year -- the thought and loving care that went into writing character backstories, roleplaying and decision-making at the table, and dedication to the campaign and each other did not go unnoticed. With that, I'm going to go cry in the bathroom.

Oh, also it looks like Tuesday is our day this week so be ready to roll at 6:30PM sharp!

“Google & Concord collaborate to successfully re-enable Enhanced Conversions after the initial implementation caused complex, technical issues on Disney’s site ($186M impact)”

The above success is just one example in a growing list of wins through our Google Enhanced Conversions partnership. Thanks to others who’ve stepped in to help with platform expertise (GTM, Tealium iQ, Adobe Launch, Ensighten) along the way! I’m looking at you, @Mike Boeser, @Alex Krumins, and @Brock Sauvage!

Hello all, we copied all the spaces that were marked with "Vladimir" in the table. They are marked in green, have the date we moved them and in the last field there is a link to Concord Confluence.

Basic permissions like groups and users we have fixed them. It remains for us to go through the internally locked pages and fix the group policies, because they are not transferred during transfer, but only to a specific user. We'll fix them tomorrow, but you might want to look at the transferred spaces.

 It turned out that everything is transferred to 100% and not as I thought only the unlocked pages, they just become visible as they are in the Authentic part after the internally locked group policies are fixed. This is good information for us because we now have complete clarity on transferring the confluence spaces and permissions and there will be no problem transferring the active client spaces.

 If they are linked to a Jira project they will need to be transferred after the Jira project transfer to preserve all links. For those that we have transferred now, after we transfer Jira we will go through the projects and manually fix the shortcut between confluence and Jira.

 If you can think of anything else, we're here.

Postscript: in the table, we have not updated the fields on sheets 2 and 3, which spaces have been migrated, etc. You can update them according to your process and how you manage it.

Desiree wanted us to set a time to transfer Space to come up with a plan, but it's different for everyone. Up to 2 GB spaces are transferred in about 30 minutes. Above this size between 1 and 3 hours per space.

• if you @ multiple people and have more than one taco in a message, it multiplies-- ie two @’s and :taco: :taco:  means you’re giving two to each @ (so you’ve given 4 tacos)
• if you react with the :taco: emoji to a message, anyone @’ed in that message will receive a taco (and counts as one of your 5)
